debug_info.goal:
V : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
m : MeasurableSpace Ω
G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V
p q : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
inst✝ : Countable V
hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P
hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P
⊢ AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P

failed_attempts:
by
    have : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      have hGP : AEMeasurable G P := hG.aemeasurable
      have hHP : AEMeasurable H P := hY.aemeasurable
      have hG' : Measurable G := hG.aemeasurable.measurable
      have hH' : Measurable H := hY.aemeasurable.measurable
      have : Measurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) := by
        simp [sup_apply]
        <;> measurable
      exact this.aemeasurable
    exact this

by
    have hG' : AEMeasurable G P := hG.hasLaw.m.1
    have hH' : AEMeasurable H P := hY.hasLaw.m.1
    have h : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      apply AEMeasurable.prod
      · exact hG'
      · exact hH'
      |>.map (fun x ↦ x ⊔ x)
      <;> intros <;> simp [SimpleGraph.sup_apply]
      <;> apply SimpleGraph_sup.measurable
      <;> simp_all [simpleGraphMeasurable_iff]
    exact h

by
    have h1 : IsBinomialRandom G p P := hG
    have h2 : IsBinomialRandom H q P := hY
    have h3 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      have h4 : AEMeasurable G P := (hasLaw.measurable_ae h1)
      have h5 : AEMeasurable H P := (hasLaw.measurable_ae h2)
      have h6 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
        refine' AEMeasurable.of_product _ (by simp [mefun_aem.infi]) _
        exact Inf_ae (h4.product_ae h5)
      exact h6
    exact h3

by
    -- Since G and H are AEMeasurable, their supremum is also AEMeasurable
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := hG.aemeasurable
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := hY.aemeasurable
    -- The supremum of two AEMeasurable functions is AEMeasurable
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      apply AEMeasurable.sup h₁ h₂
    -- Therefore, (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) is AEMeasurable
    exact h₃

by
    have h1 : AEMeasurable G P := hG.aemeasurable
    have h2 : AEMeasurable H P := hY.aemeasurable
    have h3 : AEMeasurable fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω P := by
      refine' AEMeasurable.congr (h1.sup h2) _
      filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem (measurableSpace Ω)] with ω
      <;> simp [inf_coe_sup]
    exact h3
  <;> simp_all [IsBinomialRandom]

by
    have h1 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω) P := by
      have h1' : HasLaw G (G(V, p)) P := hG
      have h2 : Measurable (fun G : SimpleGraph V => (G : SimpleGraph V)) := by simp [measurable_id]
      have h3 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω) P := by
        have h4 : HasLaw G (G(V, p)) P := hG
        have h5 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω) P := by
          apply HasLaw.aeMeasurable
          exact hG
        exact h5
      exact h3
    have h2 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => H ω) P := by
      have h2' : HasLaw H (G(V, q)) P := hY
      have h2 : Measurable (fun H : SimpleGraph V => (H : SimpleGraph V)) := by simp [measurable_id]
      have h3 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => H ω) P := by
        have h4 : HasLaw H (G(V, q)) P := hY
        have h5 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => H ω) P := by
          apply HasLaw.aeMeasurable
          exact hY
        exact h5
      exact h3
    have h3 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the supremum of two measurable functions is measurable
      have h4 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
        apply AEMeasurable.sup
        · exact h1
        · exact h2
      exact h4
    exact h3

by
    have h1 : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
      have h2 : AEMeasurable G P := hG.aemeasurable
      have h3 : AEMeasurable H P := hY.aemeasurable
      -- The union of two measurable functions is measurable
      have h4 : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P := by
        refine' AEMeasurable.mono _ (AEMeasurable.prod h2 h3)
        -- The union operation is measurable
        intro ω
        exact (inferInstance : Measurable (fun p : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => p.1 ⊔ p.2))
      exact h4
    exact h1
  
  <;> try simp_all [IsBinomialRandom]
  <;> try norm_num
  <;> try aesop

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := hG.2
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := hY.2
    apply AEMeasurable.sup h₁ h₂
